mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day forward returns occur in >65% of periods when FEDFUNDS increases, or rolling Rank IC stays below 0.02 across all 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 5.0%, it signals a deteriorating labor market that reduces consumer spending capacity and corporate profit growth, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >60% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 5.0%, or the signal shows no statistical edge over a buy-and-hold baseline.

mechanism_narrative: Strong retail sales growth combined with stable or declining federal funds rates indicates robust consumer demand supported by accommodative financing, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform individual series signals, or positive returns occur in <45% of triggered windows despite favorable macro conditions.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production signals expanding manufacturing capacity and rising corporate output, which translates to positive short-term equity momentum over the next week during expansionary phases.
failure_condition: INDPRO acceleration yields negative 5-day forward returns in >55% of cases, or the signal loses predictive power when GDP growth exceeds 3.0% annually.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects a strengthening economy and higher aggregate wages, which supports corporate revenue growth and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Payroll growth fails to correlate with forward returns, with Rank IC remaining below 0.01 across all rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly drop in housing starts indicates a contracting construction sector and weakening consumer confidence, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: HOUST declines by more than 5% month-over-month
failure_condition: Housing starts declines fail to predict negative equity returns, with positive 20-day returns occurring in >60% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Rising interest rates coinciding with increasing unemployment create a stagflationary pressure that compresses profit margins and equity valuations, resulting in negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The combined signal produces positive forward returns in >50% of cases, or the interaction term shows no incremental predictive value over linear models.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating real GDP growth signals strengthening economic momentum that boosts corporate earnings expectations, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next week during early expansion phases.
failure_condition: GDP acceleration fails to predict positive 5-day returns, with negative outcomes occurring in >60% of instances during the specified regime.
